creator: uqs@FreeBSD.org
text:
Jo Ed, what's up with this?

On -CURRENT I now get mutt's what-key to output the following

FreeBSD/xterm/TERM=xterm-256color
Char = <Home>, Octal = 406, Decimal = 262
Char = <End>, Octal = 550, Decimal = 360

FreeBSD/xterm+tmux/TERM=screen or FreeBSD/xterm+screen/TERM=screen
Char = <Home>, Octal = 406, Decimal = 262
Char = <End>, Octal = 550, Decimal = 360

FreeBSD/xterm+tmux/TERM=xterm-256color or FreeBSD/xterm+screen/TERM=xterm-256color
Char = ~, Octal = 176, Decimal = 126
Char = ~, Octal = 176, Decimal = 126
(Ins,Del,PgUp,PgDn are all fine, however!)


Why is the xterm termcap poison for tmux and screen? Is this behavior expected and why is Home/End special in this regard?
